info.name: Penneo API
info.description:
A collection for validating the Penneo API before integrating. Covers authentication, sending a casefile for signing, polling job status, checking casefile status, and retrieving documents.

**Setup**
Add the following variables to this collection before running:
- `clientId` — your OAuth client ID
- `clientSecret` — your OAuth client secret
- `apiKey` — your Penneo API key
- `apiSecret` — your Penneo API secret

The `baseUrl` and `authUrl` are pre-configured for sandbox. Change them to the production URLs when you are ready to go live.

**Flow**
Run the requests in order — each one automatically saves the values needed by the next.

variable.description: Base URL for the Penneo API. Change to https://app.penneo.com for production.
Base URL for the Penneo OAuth server. Change to https://login.penneo.com for production.
Auto-set by request 3 once the job completes. Can also be set manually.
Auto-set by request 4 to the first document ID. Update manually to download a specific document.
Auto-set by request 4 as a JSON array of all document IDs in the casefile.

exec: throw new Error('Authentication failed: Missing required collection variables (clientId, clientSecret, apiKey, apiSecret).');
// 3. Calculate digest: SHA1(nonce + createdAt + apiSecret), Base64-encoded
console.log('Access token saved. Expires in ' + json.expires_in + ' seconds.');
console.error('No access token in response:', JSON.stringify(json));

request.description:
Authenticates using the API Keys Grant flow. The pre-request script automatically calculates the nonce, timestamp, and SHA1 digest — no manual setup needed beyond setting your collection variables.

The access token is valid for 10 minutes and is automatically saved to `accessToken` for use in subsequent requests.

formdata.description: JSON string describing the casefile. The document name must exactly match the filename of the uploaded file.
Select your PDF file here. The filename must match the 'name' field in the data JSON above.

request.description:
Creates a new casefile with one or more documents and signers.

The request uses `multipart/form-data` with two parts:
- `data`: a JSON string describing the casefile (title, signers, documents)
- `files`: the PDF file(s) to be signed

**Important:** The `name` field in the documents array must exactly match the filename of the uploaded PDF.

The casefile is created asynchronously. Use the returned `uuid` and `payloadHash` (auto-saved) to poll for completion in request 3.

Authentication uses `X-Auth-Token` (not `Authorization: Bearer`) — this is specific to the send endpoint.

exec: console.log('Job not yet complete — keep polling.');

request.description:
Polls the status of the casefile creation job submitted in request 2.

Possible statuses:
- `pending` — job is queued, not yet started
- `processing` — job is being processed
- `completed` — casefile created successfully
- `failed` — something went wrong (see `errorMessage`)
- `aborted_duplicated` — a duplicate submission was detected

This endpoint does not require authentication — the `uuid` and `payloadHash` together serve as verification.

Rate limited to 20 requests per minute. Re-run this request until the status is `completed`.

exec: console.log('Multiple documents found (' + ids.length + '). All IDs saved to documentIds: ' + JSON.stringify(ids));
console.log('documentId is set to the first: ' + ids[0] + '. Update it manually for other documents, or use the Collection Runner to iterate.');
console.log('No documents found in casefile response.');

request.description:
Retrieves the full details of a casefile by ID, including its status and the status of each signer.

Signer statuses:
- `0` Request sent
- `1` Request opened
- `2` Opened
- `3` Signed
- `4` Rejected

Casefile statuses:
- `0` Draft
- `1` Pending
- `2` Rejected
- `3` Deleted
- `5` Completed
- `7` Expired

The first document ID is automatically saved to `documentId` for use in request 5.
